regimens.site: Solitary plasmacytoma or solitary plasmacytoma with minimal marrow involvement
regimens.setting: Primary
regimens.notes: Radiation therapy is the intervention of choice for solitary plasmacytoma. Recommended RT dose: 40–50 Gy in 1.8–2.0 Gy fractions (20–25 total fractions) to the involved site. For solitary plasmacytomas <5 cm in size, treatment with 35–40 Gy is an acceptable alternative due to high local control rates. Consider surgery if structurally unstable or if there is neurologic compromise due to mass effect. Reassess after at least 3 months following radiation as assessment of response with imaging may not be accurate sooner. All plasmacytomas should be imaged yearly, preferably with the same technique used at diagnosis, for at least 5 years.

regimens.setting: Primary
regimens.notes: Daratumumab monotherapy improved 5-year PFS compared to active monitoring in patients with high-risk smoldering myeloma. In subgroup analysis by 20/2/20 criteria, the PFS benefit was clear in patients with high-risk SMM (HR, 0.36; 95% CI, 0.23–0.58) but did not reach statistical significance in those with intermediate-risk or low-risk SMM.

regimens.setting: Primary

trial_data.pfs: TTP not reached vs. 23 months (HR, 0.24; 95% CI, 0.14–0.41)

regimens.notes: Treatment with lenalidomide and dexamethasone delayed median time to progression to symptomatic disease compared to no treatment. However, the flow cytometry-based high-risk criteria specified in the study are not uniformly available and participants did not receive advanced imaging.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for HCT candidates

drugs.name: Daratumumab
drugs.schedule: Weekly for cycles 1-3, then every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Bortezomib
drugs.schedule: Days 1, 8, 15, 22 of each 28-day cycle for 4 cycles

drugs.name: Lenalidomide
drugs.schedule: Days 1-14 of each 28-day cycle for 4 cycles

drugs.name: Dexamethasone
drugs.schedule: Days 1, 8, 15, 22 of each 28-day cycle for 4 cycles

regimens.notes: Based on the PERSEUS trial, addition of daratumumab to VRd backbone during induction, consolidation, and to lenalidomide maintenance significantly improved progression free survival and depth of response. After at least 24 months of maintenance therapy, daratumumab maintenance was discontinued in patients who had a CR or better and had sustained MRD–negative status for at least 12 months.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for HCT candidates

drugs.name: Isatuximab-irfc
drugs.schedule: Weekly for 4 cycles, then every 2 weeks

drugs.name: Bortezomib
drugs.schedule: Days 1, 8, 15, 22 of each 42-day cycle for 3 cycles

drugs.name: Lenalidomide
drugs.schedule: Days 1-21 of each 42-day cycle for 3 cycles

drugs.name: Dexamethasone
drugs.schedule: Days 1, 2, 8, 9, 15, 16, 22, 23 of each 42-day cycle for 3 cycles

trial_data.pfs: PFS not reached in either arm (HR, 0.70; 95% CI, 0.52–0.95; P = .0184)

regimens.notes: The 18-week induction with Isa-VRd therapy significantly prolonged PFS compared with VRd, regardless of maintenance therapy. Higher incidences of grade 3 or 4 neutropenia (23% vs. 7%) and grade 3 or 4 infections (12% vs. 10%) were observed.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for HCT candidates

trial_data.os: Median OS: >84 months vs. 69 months (HR, 0.709; 95% CI, 0.524–0.959)

regimens.notes: The three-drug regimen (VRd) showed longer PFS and improved OS compared to lenalidomide/dexamethasone. With longer-term follow-up (median, 84 months), the benefits of adding bortezomib were maintained.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for HCT candidates
regimens.notes: The phase III ENDURANCE trial showed similar PFS with KRd versus VRd. KRd was associated with less neuropathy but more dyspnea, hypertension, heart failure, and acute kidney injury compared with VRd.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for HCT candidates

drugs.name: Daratumumab
drugs.schedule: Weekly for cycles 1-2, then every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: The MASTER trial used MRD status to determine additional consolidation after autologous HCT. MRD negativity (<10−5) was seen in 81% with no high-risk chromosomal abnormalities (HRCA).

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for HCT candidates

drugs.name: Isatuximab-irfc
drugs.schedule: Weekly for 4 cycles, then every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Isa-KRd significantly increased MRD negativity rates after induction and consolidation phases compared to KRd.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for HCT candidates
regimens.notes: CyBorD is an option for patients with acute renal insufficiency who do not have access to daratumumab. Consider switching to dara-VRd after renal function improves.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for HCT candidates

trial_data.trial_name: Pooled analysis of phase I/II studies
trial_data.pfs: Median PFS: 35.7 months (weekly) vs. 35.5 months (twice weekly)

regimens.notes: Carfilzomib/cyclophosphamide/dexamethasone is an option for patients with renal insufficiency and/or peripheral neuropathy.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for HCT candidates

drugs.name: Daratumumab
drugs.schedule: Weekly for cycles 1-2, then every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: This regimen is a treatment option for patients with renal insufficiency or acute kidney injury (AKI). Consider switching to dara-RVd after renal function improves.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy for HCT candidates
regimens.notes: Generally reserved for the treatment of aggressive MM, such as high-risk and aggressive extramedullary disease or plasma cell leukemia.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy if HCT deferred or if HCT is not indicated

drugs.name: Daratumumab
drugs.schedule: Weekly for cycles 1-2, then every 2 weeks

trial_data.pfs: Median PFS: 61.9 months vs. 34.4 months (HR, 0.55; 95% CI, 0.45–0.67; P < .0001)
trial_data.os: Median OS: not reached vs. 65.5 months (HR, 0.66; 95% CI, 0.53–0.83; P = .0003)

regimens.notes: The addition of daratumumab to lenalidomide/dexamethasone resulted in significantly longer PFS and OS in HCT-ineligible patients with NDMM. D-Rd arm also demonstrated higher rates of CR or better, MRD negativity, and sustained MRD negativity.

regimens.notes: KRd is well tolerated and effective in all patients with NDMM, including those who are not eligible for HCT.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy if HCT deferred or if HCT is not indicated

drugs.name: Isatuximab-irfc
drugs.schedule: Weekly for 4 cycles, then every 2 weeks

trial_data.mrd: MRD negativity rate at 18 months: 53% vs. 26% (OR, 3.16; 95% CI, 1.89–5.28; P < .0001)

regimens.notes: Isa-Rd is an option in the setting of peripheral neuropathy. The BENEFIT trial compared triplet vs. quadruplet therapy with isatuximab.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy if HCT deferred or if HCT is not indicated

trial_data.pfs: Risk of progression or death reduced by 28% (HR, 0.72; 95% CI, 0.61–0.85; P < .001) vs. MPT

regimens.notes: Consider treatment with continuous lenalidomide/dexamethasone until disease progression for select patients who are not eligible for HCT. Appropriate thromboprophylaxis is required.

regimens.setting: Primary therapy if HCT deferred or if HCT is not indicated
